title: leontodon crispus subsp. saxatilis
intro: Leontodon crispus subsp. saxatilis, also known as the Rock Hawksbit or Rock Hawksbeard, is a subspecies of the Leontodon crispus plant, a member of the dandelion family, native to Europe and western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects. Leontodon crispus subsp. saxatilis has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its availability and use may vary due to its rarity and limited scientific research.
